GS3.imageDescription: A stylized graphic showing the lifecycle of plastic in a circular economy, with arrows for reuse and recycling.
GS3.trivia: India generates approximately 3.5 million tonnes of plastic waste annually, with a significant portion remaining uncollected and polluting land and water bodies.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful test of its indigenously developed Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) demonstrator.
• The test involved a precise landing experiment mimicking an orbital re-entry, demonstrating critical technologies for future two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) reusable vehicles.
• This achievement is a significant step towards reducing the cost of space access and enhancing India's competitive edge in the global space market.
• The RLV program aims to develop a fully reusable launch system, capable of multiple missions with minimal refurbishment, similar to space shuttle concepts.
• Future plans include further atmospheric re-entry tests and orbital flights, paving the way for more frequent and affordable space missions.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully tested Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) demonstrator.
Test involved a precise landing experiment.
Aims to reduce cost of space access and achieve TSTO capability.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India's space program and its implications for commercial space exploration.
Analyze how ISRO's indigenous development of such technologies contributes to national self-reliance and global competitiveness.
Examine the economic and environmental benefits of reusable launch systems compared to traditional expendable rockets.
Evaluate the challenges and future prospects for India in developing a fully operational reusable space transportation system.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) gliding back to Earth.
GS3.trivia: ISRO successfully conducted the first RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) mission, HEX (Hypersonic Flight Experiment), in 2016, marking a key milestone.

GS3.headline: India's Q1 GDP Growth Exceeds Expectations, Driven by Manufacturing and Services
GS3.explainer:
• India's Gross Domestic Product (GDP) growth for the first quarter (April-June) of the fiscal year 2025-26 surpassed analyst expectations.
• The National Statistical Office (NSO) reported a robust growth rate, primarily propelled by strong performance in the manufacturing, construction, and services sectors.
• Private consumption expenditure showed a significant uptick, indicating improving consumer confidence and demand in the economy.
• Government capital expenditure continued to provide crucial support for infrastructure development, contributing to overall economic momentum.
• The positive data reinforces India's position as one of the fastest-growing major economies, despite global headwinds, bolstering investor sentiment.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Investment models
prelimsPoints: Q1 GDP growth (April-June FY25-26) exceeded expectations.
Key drivers: Manufacturing, Construction, Services.
Significant uptick in private consumption expenditure.
Reported by National Statistical Office (NSO).
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to India's robust economic growth in the current fiscal year, especially the roles of manufacturing and services.
Discuss the challenges that India's economy might face in sustaining this growth trajectory amidst global uncertainties.
Examine the relationship between government capital expenditure, private consumption, and overall economic growth.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current economic policies in fostering inclusive and sustainable growth.
GS3.imageDescription: A bar graph showing upward trend in economic growth, with indicators for manufacturing and services.
GS3.trivia: The National Statistical Office (NSO) is the statistical arm of the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI) and is responsible for national income accounting.

GS3.headline: India and Bangladesh Conduct Joint Naval Exercise 'Samudra Maitri' for Maritime Security
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Navy and Bangladesh Navy commenced their joint bilateral naval exercise, 'Samudra Maitri', in the Bay of Bengal.
• The exercise focuses on enhancing interoperability, sharing best practices, and strengthening cooperation in maritime security, search and rescue operations, and anti-piracy efforts.
• It involves a series of complex drills, including anti-submarine warfare, surface warfare, and communication exercises, simulating various maritime scenarios.
• This annual exercise underscores the growing strategic partnership between India and Bangladesh, particularly in ensuring security and stability in the Indian Ocean Region.
• The collaboration aims to counter traditional and non-traditional maritime threats and foster a secure environment for trade and navigation.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: Joint naval exercise 'Samudra Maitri' between India and Bangladesh.
Location: Bay of Bengal.
Focus: Maritime security, search and rescue, anti-piracy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of bilateral naval exercises like 'Samudra Maitri' in strengthening India's maritime security strategy and its 'Act East' policy.
Analyze the evolving maritime security challenges in the Bay of Bengal and the broader Indian Ocean Region.
Examine how defense cooperation between India and Bangladesh contributes to regional stability and counters common threats.
Evaluate the role of joint exercises in enhancing interoperability and preparedness among navies for humanitarian assistance and disaster relief (HADR) operations.
GS3.imageDescription: Warships from India and Bangladesh sailing in formation during a joint naval exercise.
GS3.trivia: Bangladesh is strategically important for India due to its geographical location, sharing a long land and maritime border, and its role in India's 'Neighborhood First' policy.

GS3.headline: Government Launches National Campaign to Combat Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR)
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, in collaboration with ICMR, launched a nationwide public awareness and intervention campaign against Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR).
• The campaign focuses on responsible use of antibiotics, promoting hygiene, improving sanitation, and strengthening surveillance of drug-resistant infections.
• It aims to educate both healthcare professionals and the general public about the dangers of antibiotic misuse and the importance of completing prescribed courses.
• The initiative is part of India's National Action Plan on AMR, aligning with global efforts to address this growing public health threat.
• Research and development for new antibiotics and alternative therapies are also being prioritized under this comprehensive strategy.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
GS3.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: National campaign against Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR) launched by Ministry of Health & FW and ICMR.

Analyze the key strategies adopted in India's National Action Plan on AMR and their potential effectiveness.
Examine the role of public awareness, healthcare professional training, and regulatory frameworks in combating antibiotic misuse.
Evaluate the 'One Health' approach in addressing AMR, considering its intersection with human, animal, and environmental health.
GS3.imageDescription: A visual representation of bacteria resisting antibiotics, symbolizing antimicrobial resistance.
GS3.trivia: AMR is considered one of the top 10 global public health threats facing humanity by the World Health Organization (WHO).

GS3.headline: SEBI Introduces Stricter Disclosure Norms for Venture Capital Funds (VCFs)
GS3.explainer:
• The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) announced new, stringent disclosure norms for Venture Capital Funds (VCFs) operating in India.
• The regulations mandate VCFs to provide more detailed information on their investment strategies, risk management, and portfolio companies to investors and the market regulator.
• This move aims to enhance transparency, protect investor interests, and bring greater accountability to the rapidly growing alternative investment fund (AIF) sector.
• SEBI also tightened rules regarding valuation methodologies and reporting standards for VCFs, ensuring fair and consistent practices.
• The new norms are expected to streamline the regulatory framework for VCFs, fostering a more robust and trustworthy environment for venture capital investments.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Investment models

Examine the challenges faced by market regulators in balancing innovation with investor protection in the alternative investment fund sector.
Evaluate how these new regulations might influence both domestic and foreign investment in Indian startups.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic illustrating financial regulations and investment growth, with SEBI logo.
GS3.trivia: SEBI is a statutory body established in 1992 under the SEBI Act, 1992, to protect the interests of investors in securities and to promote the development of, and to regulate the securities market.

GS3.headline: National Mission on Natural Farming Shows Promising Progress, Expands to More States
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare released a progress report on the National Mission on Natural Farming (NMNF), highlighting significant achievements.
• The mission, launched to promote chemical-free, climate-resilient agriculture, has expanded its outreach to 15 additional states and Union Territories.
• Data indicates a substantial increase in farmer adoption, improved soil health parameters, and reduced input costs for participating farmers.
• Training and capacity-building programs, along with the provision of organic inputs, have been key to the mission's success.
• NMNF aims to further scale up natural farming practices, contributing to sustainable agriculture, food security, and farmers' income enhancement.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System- objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
GS3.source: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare

Expanded to 15 additional states/UTs.

Promotes chemical-free, climate-resilient agriculture.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of natural farming practices in addressing the challenges of conventional agriculture, including soil degradation and climate change.

GS3.imageDescription: A farmer tending to a lush, green field, symbolizing natural and organic farming practices.
GS3.trivia: Subhash Palekar, an Indian agriculturist, is credited with developing the concept of Zero Budget Natural Farming (ZBNF), a popular approach within natural farming.

GS3.headline: Indian Railways Inaugurates First Section of Dedicated High-Speed Freight Corridor
GS3.explainer:
• Indian Railways officially inaugurated the first operational section of its ambitious Dedicated High-Speed Freight Corridor (DHSFC).
• The 250-km stretch, connecting major industrial hubs, is designed exclusively for freight trains, aiming to significantly reduce transit times and logistics costs.
• The DHSFC project is a cornerstone of India's infrastructure development, enhancing freight movement efficiency and decongesting existing railway lines.
• It features state-of-the-art signaling systems, higher axle load capacity tracks, and electrification, allowing for faster and heavier cargo movement.
• This development is expected to boost industrial growth, improve supply chain reliability, and contribute to India's economic competitiveness.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment

mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Dedicated Freight Corridors (DFCs) in transforming India's logistics sector and boosting economic growth.

Examine the challenges involved in the planning, financing, and execution of large-scale infrastructure projects like the DHSFC.
Evaluate how improved railway infrastructure can contribute to India's manufacturing competitiveness and 'Make in India' initiative.
GS3.imageDescription: A modern, high-speed freight train moving along a dedicated track, symbolizing efficient logistics.
GS3.trivia: The Dedicated Freight Corridor Corporation of India Limited (DFCCIL) is a special purpose vehicle (SPV) established by the Ministry of Railways to undertake planning, development, and construction of DFCs.
